how_it_works: Blight Oils are drops from Blight encounters. They are used to Anoint items — applying a Notable passive skill to an Amulet (3 oils) or a small passive bonus to a Ring (2 oils). Oils are also used to upgrade Blighted Maps with additional rewards. Higher-tier oils produce more powerful anointments.

anoint_rules.amulet: Combines 3 oils to anoint any Notable passive skill directly onto the amulet. The passive is granted without needing to path to it on the Skill Tree.
anoint_rules.ring: Combines 2 oils to add a small passive bonus (life, resistances, damage) to a ring. Weaker than amulet anoints.
anoint_rules.blighted_map: Upgrading a Blighted Map with oils increases its rewards and enemy difficulty. Higher oils grant more dangerous but more lucrative maps.

tips: Notable anoints from Cassia (the Blight NPC) let you skip long paths on the Skill Tree — high-value for late-game keystones.
The exact 3-oil combination for each Notable passive can be looked up on poe.trade or the Anoint Calculator.
Clear and Sepia Oils are extremely common — save Silver, Golden, and Opalescent Oils for high-value anoints.
Opalescent Oil is the rarest — only used for the most powerful Notable passives (e.g. Eldritch Battery, Resolute Technique, Glancing Blows).
Anointing a Blighted Map with Golden Oil gives guaranteed Blight-ravaged Maps and increased rewards, making it the best investment for dedicated Blight farming.
Rings can be anointed multiple times — each anoint consumes both oils and removes the previous anoint.

oils.name: Clear Oil
oils.notes: The most common drop from Blight. Used in the cheapest amulet anointments (small stats) and very basic ring anoints.

oils.name: Sepia Oil
oils.notes: Almost as common as Clear Oil. Slightly higher-tier anoints. Often dropped in bulk — use freely.

oils.name: Amber Oil
oils.notes: Begins to appear in medium-tier Notable anoints. Still drops frequently enough that most players accumulate surplus.

oils.name: Verdant Oil
oils.notes: Notable anoints requiring Verdant Oil are moderate-tier (e.g. basic life cluster passives). Constitution anoint uses 3× Verdant.

oils.name: Teal Oil
oils.notes: Mid-tier oil used for meaningful defensive and offensive Notable anoints.

oils.name: Violet Oil
oils.notes: Starts to appear in high-demand anoints. Balance stockpiling vs. spending on anoints you actually need.

oils.name: Crimson Oil
oils.notes: Frequently required for powerful damage Notable anoints. Used in Blighted Map upgrades for mid-tier rewards.

oils.name: Black Oil
oils.notes: Used in high-value Notable anoints. Drops much less frequently — trade for them if needed for a specific build.

oils.name: Silver Oil
oils.notes: High-tier anointments only. Required for strong keystones like Zealot's Oath. Worth purchasing from trade for key anoints.

oils.name: Golden Oil
oils.notes: Near-top tier. Used for the strongest accessible Notable passives. Also the go-to oil for upgrading Blighted Maps to maximum rewards.

oils.name: Opalescent Oil
oils.notes: The rarest oil. Required for the most powerful Notable passives — Eldritch Battery, Resolute Technique, Glancing Blows, Iron Reflexes. Trade for specific builds if needed.

notable_anoints.name: Constitution
notable_anoints.notes: Best life anoint for low-to-mid budget builds. 3× Verdant Oils are cheap.

notable_anoints.name: Zealot's Oath
notable_anoints.effect: Life Regeneration applies to Energy Shield instead of Life
notable_anoints.notes: Core anoint for Energy Shield builds that want Life Regeneration to heal their ES pool.

notable_anoints.name: Eldritch Battery
notable_anoints.effect: Spend Energy Shield before Mana for Skill Costs. Energy Shield does not recharge during Flask Effect.
notable_anoints.notes: Core keystone for some ES-based Mana builds. Extremely expensive to anoint — buy the amulet with it already if possible.

notable_anoints.name: Resolute Technique
notable_anoints.effect: Your hits can never be Evaded. Never deal Critical Strikes.
notable_anoints.notes: Max cost (3× Opalescent). Core for melee builds that struggle with accuracy but do not rely on crits.

notable_anoints.name: Iron Reflexes
notable_anoints.effect: Converts all Evasion Rating to Armour. Dexterity no longer provides bonus Evasion.
notable_anoints.notes: Transforms Evasion stackers into extreme Armour builds. Core for specific Determination + Grace hybrid builds.

notable_anoints.name: Glancing Blows
notable_anoints.effect: Chance to Block Attack Damage and Spell Damage is doubled, but Blocks only prevent 50% of damage.
notable_anoints.notes: Key for block-capped builds that can tolerate taking 50% of blocked hits. Enables near 100% effective block.

notable_anoints.name: Divine Flesh
notable_anoints.effect: 50% of Elemental Damage you take is taken as Chaos Damage instead
notable_anoints.notes: Requires very high Chaos resistance (usually 75%+) to be safe. Core for certain tanky defensive builds.

notable_anoints.name: Practical Application
notable_anoints.effect: Grants a Curse Socket (can apply an additional Curse on enemies)
notable_anoints.notes: Effectively a free curse slot (equivalent to Doedre's Damning ring). Frees up a ring slot or enables triple-curse builds.

notable_anoints.name: Hired Killer
notable_anoints.notes: Best damage anoint for Minion builds at a reasonable oil cost.

notable_anoints.name: Persistence
notable_anoints.effect: 20% chance to gain a Flask Charge when you deal a Killing Blow
notable_anoints.notes: Cheap anoint for flask-dependent builds that clear large packs.

notable_anoints.name: Alira's Reward
notable_anoints.effect: Regenerate 3 Mana per second; +15% to all Elemental Resistances; 5% increased Mana Regeneration
notable_anoints.notes: Very cheap anoint providing a small but useful resistance and mana bonus. Good for new characters needing resistance caps.
